post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 249 (Newbie Game 98)

Ruleset heavily inspired by fdas
Important Rules:
Do not edit or delete posts
Do not post during night
Do not post if you are dead or if you are not in the game
Do not talk about anything related to this game outside this thread
The Mafia can talk anytime!
Read all the rules!


Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#00FF00), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in code tags in the format 
      [code]Vote: <insert player here>[/‎code]

      Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. A proper vote looks like 
      Vote: fdas
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post 
      Vote: No Hang
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format 
      Vote: Unvote

      An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Even if you misspell a username, as long as it looks like you are voting for a player, your vote will be counted.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 240 hours (10 days). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 240 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 48 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive PM prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.10: If a player receives 4 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.11: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I (midwaybear) am the moderator of Mafia Game 249. MaverickMan is the co-moderator.
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.7: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game will use the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons


Role PMs
Quote:
You are an Ordinary Mountaineer (Vanilla Townie).
You and your crew set off to climb a mountain. You'd climbed before, but you had never climbed a mountain this tall, you'd never gone this high. You were going to scale Mount Everest. It was a massive task to undertake, but you believed you were up to it. However, after the first night at camp, someone goes missing. You don't know what's happened, or where they are, but you thought you'd leave it to the authorities on the ground to catch them. Unfortunately, the murderers turned out to be a part of your crew. You don't know what they're doing or why, but you plan to put an end to it.

You have no active abilities. You win when all of the Selfish/Greedy Mountaineers (Mafia) have been eliminated.

Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.
Quote:
You are a Sherpa (Cop).

You've helped many people climb Mount Everest. You know what you're doing. As one of the locals, you're accustomed to the harsh weather. What you aren't accustomed to is having a murderer on the mountain. As your crew discovers that two of the people are killing the others, one by one, you decide to take matters into your own hands. Every day, you'll take a look at one person, and figure out whether or not they're one of the killers on the mountain.

Each night, you may target a player. Assuming no interference with your action, you will learn whether that player is aligned with the town. You will get "no result" if this action is blocked. You win when all of the Selfish/Greedy Mountaineers (Mafia) have been eliminated.

Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.
Quote:
You are a Medic (Doctor). You're the least experienced of the mountaineers. You aren't unexperienced, but you aren't quite as good at it. However, that doesn't mean you're totally useless. Along with you, you bring medical equipment. Every night, you'll go around, and go into someone's tent, making sure they're safe. If they aren't, you'll quickly bandage them up, and do your best to save them.

Each night, you may target a player. Assuming no interference with your action, that player will be protected from one kill that night. You win when all of the Selfish/Greedy Mountaineers (Mafia) have been eliminated.

Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.
Quote:
You are a Night Watcher (Tracker). You're suspicious. You know someone is killing everyone, and you want to figure out who. At this point, you've resorted to stalking them. You're keeping track of where just one person goes, each night. You watch their tracks, and figure out where they go, if anywhere. You don't know what they did, just that they did something, and who they did it to.

Each night, you may target a player. Assuming no interference with your action, you will learn who that player targeted that night. You will get "no result" if this action is blocked. You win when all of the Selfish/Greedy Mountaineers (Mafia) have been eliminated.

Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.
Quote:
You are a Tent Constructor (Jailkeeper). For the journey up to the summit of Everest, you build, setup, and carry all the tents. You know each one of them, inside, and out. Literally. Because of this, you know how to rig them shut. It makes it much harder to enter - although, it can be done - and nearly impossible to exit. This makes sure that the person inside the tent can't do anything. Additionally, your hope is that the killers will panic at the possibility of being caught when loudly opening the securely shut tent, and will leave to avoid being caught. That might be a bit of a stretch, though.

Each night, you may target a player. Assuming no interference with your action, that player will be protected from kills that night, but all their actions (if they have any) will be blocked that night. You win when all of the Selfish/Greedy Mountaineers (Mafia) have been eliminated.

Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.
Quote:
You are an Experienced Mountaineer (1-Shot Bulletproof Townie). You've been climbing mountains longer than you could remember. You're pretty sure you first scaled Aconcagua when you were three years old. Over time, you've gotten pretty used to everything. You know how to climb, you know what to do. This will be your second time climbing Mount Everest. As you get used to things, you've decided to start getting some important tools to bring along with you. One of them is a bulletproof vest. It'll work once, but once the murderers figure it out, they'll just go for the head. You hope this is enough to save you from being killed, but you aren't totally sure. Nevertheless, you're continuing to go along with the crew - even if there's a chance of being killed.

You are protected from the first kill that hits you. You win when all of the Selfish/Greedy Mountaineers (Mafia) have been eliminated.

Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.
Quote:
You are a Selfish Mountaineer (Mafia Goon). You're a bit selfish. Scratch that, you're extremely selfish. While you climb Mt. Everest, you're hoping to, along with your equally narcissistic friend, kill the other mountaineers. You want to kill them for the obvious reason that you'd prefer if the glory of successfully summiting this mountain went to you, rather than those other mountaineers. Who wouldn't? So, every night, while you climb the mountain, you or your friend goes off and kills one of the other mountaineers. When you reach the summit, you'll be able to tell the incredible story of being the only two mountaineers left alive after a surprisingly dangerous trip up the mountain.

You may talk with your partner here: .Each night, you and your teammate () may choose a player to kill, and who performs the action. You win when all of the Mountaineers (Town) are dead (or if nothing can prevent this from happening).

Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.
Quote:
You are an Greedy Mountaineer (Mafia Roleblocker).

You're greedy. Extremely so. Maybe not quite as much as your friend, but you're greedy. Being greedy, you two sometimes just... kill people. Now, this sounds deranged, but it's just because you want the glory of climbing a treacherous mountain reserved for yourself, and maybe your friend as well. Actually, wait, that is deranged. Still, you don't plan to let that stop you from doing it. Now, every night, you or your friend will find someone who you aim to go off and kill. In addition to that, you go off each night, and just kinda... keep someone's door shut. If they can't get out of their tent, they can't do anything.

You may talk with your partner here: .Each night, you and your teammate () may choose a player to kill, and who performs the action. Additionally, each night, you may choose a player to roleblock. That player may not perform any actions that night. You win when all of the Mountaineers (Town) are dead (or if nothing can prevent this from happening).

Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.

Do not post until this line is crossed out

A group of mountaineers were getting ready for the moment of their lifetimes. They were getting ready to climb Mount Everest. The morning after they slept for a night at base camp, though, something was wrong. They woke up to find someone... missing. No one was really sure what to do, but they assumed someone had needed to go back, or something, and so, they continued on. To be sure, though, they told the authorities. Just in case something was worse. It turned out that something was worse. People were dropping dead every night. So, they formed a system. Every day, they would vote on one person to kick out of their group. If they were right, they were right. If they were wrong, they were in a terrible situation. Unfortunately, they didn't have a better system to use, so that's what they did. Night by night, day by day, they risked their lives on the tallest peak on Earth.

Flavor by MaverickMan.

A group of mountaineers were getting ready for the moment of their lifetimes. They were getting ready to climb Mount Everest. The morning after they slept for a night at base camp, though, something was wrong. They woke up to find someone... missing. No one was really sure what to do, but they assumed someone had needed to go back, or something, and so, they continued on. To be sure, though, they told the authorities. Just in case something was worse. It turned out that something was worse. People were dropping dead every night. So, they formed a system. Every day, they would vote on one person to kick out of their group. If they were right, they were right. If they were wrong, they were in a terrible situation. Unfortunately, they didn't have a better system to use, so that's what they did. Night by night, day by day, they risked their lives on the tallest peak on Earth.

Flavor by MaverickMan.

FAQ

1. Can I be an annoying prick and act like I'm right and no one else is when I'm really not?

No.

Mafia is a team game, and frankly, you're new to this. Trust us, even if we're mafia, we're still here to help you. We won't lie to you and have you learn mafia wrong just for the sake of winning - that's not why we're in this game (me, hannah, and sudz).

2. Can I be an annoying prick in general?

No.

3. Can I engage in flamewars with annoying pricks?

No. Tell them they're wrong and then proceed to ignore them while someone else deals with it.

4. How can I tell someone is an annoying prick?

You just do. If you're not quite sure, I'd recommend getting scheduling them a doctor's appointment to get diagnosed. Maybe they just are 3 year olds and are biologically incapable of being anything but egocentric.

5. What if I'm an annoying prick?

No.

post_rendered:
Probably Something Worth Reading

There are 2 mafia in this game. Our job is to hang them.

There are 2 ways to do this.

a. Catch the mafia.

Well, be on the lookout for things that are mafia-indicative. Hang people who do those things. Be right.

b. Force the mafia into a box.

Mafia win when they achieve parity, essentially, when there are the same number of townies as mafia. This means that, at least for this setup, mafia need to get town to hang other townies 3 times - essentially, we will almost always lose if we hang wrong 3 times.

This means we can be wrong a maximum of 2 times before we lose - and as there are 2 mafia, this means the maximum number of people we can have in our box is 4 in order to win.

The box means you don't have to catch mafia, you just catch town and once you correctly find enough town, we'll have enough room for mistakes that even if we haven't found mafia, we can kill everyone in the box and still win - we call this box PoE (process of elimination). For this game in particular, as 4 people is how many people fit in our box, we need to find 9-4 = 5 townies in order to win.

That's not to say you can't do both, frankly, that's what you should be doing, but I personally would rely more on POE, it's so much easier to find townies than mafia, and in fact, you already know one, yourself! (so you really only have to find 4 more)
This game, I want all of you to not be annoying pricks. Unfortunately, this can be a problem. I hope that won't be one this game.

When I say annoying prick, I'm referring to the following characteristics

1. Refuses to consider other people's perspectives.
2. Gets hung up on their attacks and starts refusing to consider the possibility the player they're attacking is in fact, town.
3. Refuses to engage in logical reasoning.
4. Refuses to believe that they're doing #3.
5. Refuses to realize we're experienced at this game, and almost always know better - while this doesn't mean we'll be right all the time, nor does it mean you should blindly trust us (besides general gameplay advice like this post), but it does mean you shouldn't take what we say lightly.

If you haven't read the setup tab in the OP go read it now please.
If you have read the setup tab, you would know that town has on its side power roles, or PRs, who are basically any town with special powers beyond talking and voting like a normal VT. Those powers like jailkeeping, doctor, tracker, etc can help identify and/or block the mafia's actions, which means the mafia want to get rid of them as soon as possible (or avoid shooting them if they're a 1-shot bp — so 1sbp please try to attract the nightkill).
Anyway, if mafia find out who these people are, they can make them pretty much useless, which is bad for us.

post_rendered:
Terms and things:
  • Things that scum tend to do:
    • bandwagoning (following the crowd)
    • lurking (not showing up)
    • pocketing (sweet talking a townie into locking them town)
    • hedging (having no definitive stance/fencesitting)
    • bussing (attacking their partner to seem not w/w, usually in the form of noncommittal attacks)
    • buddying (opposite of bussing for the purpose of making sure their partner is tr'ed, I think).
    • Making up random nonsense that derails things subtlely.
  • Motivation reads basically involves questioning yourself: is scum or town more likely to say/do this/that. Be aware of the assumptions you make when you make these kinds of reads.
  • Terms to know:
    • w/w (wolf/wolf. In other words, two people are scum together.)
    • v/v (villager/villager. Two people are town together.)
    • w/v (self explanatory)
    • AI (alignment indicative)
    • NAI (not alignment indicative)
    • reads (thoughts on who is scum or town)
    • RVS (random voting stage: When people randomly vote to create something to discuss.)

HannahW wrote:
Would anyone like to initiate a joe read.
The fact that Hannah had this thought at the time they did has me believing they're town.

Up to this point, joe has posted 3 times.

All of these posts are townie for a newbie, because again, it's demonstrating that they're curious and want to solve the game. (Never mind the fact they've had irl experience, that wasn't mentioned up till this point.)

However, I personally felt like it was one of those borderline "this is pretty towny for a newbie but is it enough to call them town?" moments, and what Hannah said has me believing they had the same thought.

And if they did have the same thought, they're town - it's a thought that you don't see genuinely coming from a scum (mafia) POV. This sort of read is called the mindmeld - where you townread someone for having the same thoughts and thought processes as you. However, you have to be careful to make sure that the shared thoughts are real and genuine, you have to make sure scum isn't making them up in order to fool you.

Now, on why I think they had the same thought as me and why I think it's genuine.

Hannah saying "would anyone like to initiate a joe read" is implying that they think there's enough info to make a joe read, however, the fact they don't offer one themselves means they're holding out on doing so for some reason.

Given the fact joe only had 3 posts, I think it's a reasonable jump to assume that they had the same thought as me - I don't see any other thoughts that really lead to the same sort of behavior that Hannah demonstrated.

I think it's genuine frankly just because of the relative complexity of this read, I don't see Hannah faking something like this, even if they're experienced.

For these reasons, I think Hannah is town with very little doubt and so I am throwing them into my towncore (remember my post on POE on putting wolves into a box?)

Right now, my towncore is {bob, hannah}.

To win this game, my towncore has to have 5 townies - right now I have 2, so if I find 3 more townies - we can hang in the 4 other people and win.

BobDBuilder321 wrote:
HannahW wrote:
Would anyone like to initiate a joe read.
The fact that Hannah had this thought at the time they did has me believing they're town.

Up to this point, joe has posted 3 times.

All of these posts are townie for a newbie, because again, it's demonstrating that they're curious and want to solve the game. (Never mind the fact they've had irl experience, that wasn't mentioned up till this point.)

However, I personally felt like it was one of those borderline "this is pretty towny for a newbie but is it enough to call them town?" moments, and what Hannah said has me believing they had the same thought.

And if they did have the same thought, they're town - it's a thought that you don't see genuinely coming from a scum (mafia) POV. This sort of read is called the mindmeld - where you townread someone for having the same thoughts and thought processes as you. However, you have to be careful to make sure that the shared thoughts are real and genuine, you have to make sure scum isn't making them up in order to fool you.

Now, on why I think they had the same thought as me and why I think it's genuine.

Hannah saying "would anyone like to initiate a joe read" is implying that they think there's enough info to make a joe read, however, the fact they don't offer one themselves means they're holding out on doing so for some reason.

Given the fact joe only had 3 posts, I think it's a reasonable jump to assume that they had the same thought as me - I don't see any other thoughts that really lead to the same sort of behavior that Hannah demonstrated.

I think it's genuine frankly just because of the relative complexity of this read, I don't see Hannah faking something like this, even if they're experienced.

For these reasons, I think Hannah is town with very little doubt and so I am throwing them into my towncore (remember my post on POE on putting wolves into a box?)

Right now, my towncore is {bob, hannah}.

To win this game, my towncore has to have 5 townies - right now I have 2, so if I find 3 more townies - we can hang in the 4 other people and win.

i think mc, joe, ct-wolf, and candy seem fairly towny. joe, for the reasons stated in a post all the way up there. mc is just a noob. ct-wolf and candy in general seem more “innocent”, if that’s a way to describe their posts. candy has no idea what’s going on, and while this could be faked, she doesn’t seem to know the game that well, so i don’t think it’s faked. ct has an interesting tone to their posts. when someone (hannah, i think?) puts ct in her towncore, ct questions why, which i think is towny, because mafia wouldn’t draw attention to themselves, or try to question why other people think they’re town. they just want to be universally accepted as town, i think, to avoid getting hanged.

post_rendered:
And I guess? yeah - it can be a bit toxic - it's not fair to act like it's not toxic at all because it can be and has been.

But, like, it's not inherently a toxic game - but emotions inevitably do come into play and people clash, feel happy, and stuff.

While most mafia games are more chill - yeah, if you can't handle some toxicity this might not be the right hobby.

The nice thing about mafia, however, is that there are objective solutions - this means most of the time, the feelings that led to said toxicity are usually justified and so there isn't much room for arguments - though it's important to make sure you don't go overboard; airing complaints and venting feelings can be fine as long as you're not making people feel really bad (making people feel bad is to some extent inevitable) and yeah.

The amount of "toxicity" I've seen this game isn't really that much so if you can't handle this much then you're probably not going to like half the mafia games you play in.

BobDBuilder321 wrote:
And I guess? yeah - it can be a bit toxic - it's not fair to act like it's not toxic at all because it can be and has been.

But, like, it's not inherently a toxic game - but emotions inevitably do come into play and people clash, feel happy, and stuff.

While most mafia games are more chill - yeah, if you can't handle some toxicity this might not be the right hobby.

The nice thing about mafia, however, is that there are objective solutions - this means most of the time, the feelings that led to said toxicity are usually justified and so there isn't much room for arguments - though it's important to make sure you don't go overboard; airing complaints and venting feelings can be fine as long as you're not making people feel really bad (making people feel bad is to some extent inevitable) and yeah.

The amount of "toxicity" I've seen this game isn't really that much so if you can't handle this much then you're probably not going to like half the mafia games you play in.
yeah I assume it's just like all other online games. I guess it's just a little amplified because there aren't clear-cut teams, so we're all suspicious of each other
BobDBuilder321 wrote:
On MC "bwing", I don't really think that's scum-indicative.

I don't think he exactly completely understands what implications that bwing carries - thus why he's open about it, he doesn't really see much of a problem with it.

This makes me think that he personally doesn't think he's doing something scummy - which tells me that his bwing didn't come from a scum mindset.

Like, bwing is only scummy if the motivation behind the bwing is to hang a townie - but MC doesn't seem to see his actions that way, he doesn't seem to think him "bwing" is scummy, which makes me think that his motivations aren't to hang a townie.

BobDBuilder321 wrote:
MC42 wrote:
in mafia it's important to share all suspicions, even the faintest ones

because as dust bunny so vehemently asserts, this isn't among us
Actually, no.

Players tend not to share gut reads - which is essentially reads based of gut, or just what a player feels like, though you might not have evidence.

And - the reason for this is simple if there's no evidence there's nothing to really discuss and the read has a good chance of being inaccurate.

On other reads that have evidence, however, yeah, it's good to share those.

Just wanted to correct that you don't have to share your faint gutreads, it's not all that important.

[quote=BobDBuilder321]And I guess? yeah - it can be a bit toxic - it's not fair to act like it's not toxic at all because it can be and has been.

But, like, it's not inherently a toxic game - but emotions inevitably do come into play and people clash, feel happy, and stuff.

While most mafia games are more chill - yeah, if you can't handle some toxicity this might not be the right hobby.

The nice thing about mafia, however, is that there are objective solutions - this means most of the time, the feelings that led to said toxicity are usually justified and so there isn't much room for arguments - though it's important to make sure you don't go overboard; airing complaints and venting feelings can be fine as long as you're not making people feel really bad (making people feel bad is to some extent inevitable) and yeah.

The amount of "toxicity" I've seen this game isn't really that much so if you can't handle this much then you're probably not going to like half the mafia games you play in.[/quote]yeah I assume it's just like all other online games. I guess it's just a little amplified because there aren't clear-cut teams, so we're all suspicious of each other[quote=BobDBuilder321]On MC "bwing", I don't really think that's scum-indicative.

I don't think he exactly completely understands what implications that bwing carries - thus why he's open about it, he doesn't really see much of a problem with it.

This makes me think that he personally doesn't think he's doing something scummy - which tells me that his bwing didn't come from a scum mindset.

Like, bwing is only scummy if the motivation behind the bwing is to hang a townie - but MC doesn't seem to see his actions that way, he doesn't seem to think him "bwing" is scummy, which makes me think that his motivations aren't to hang a townie.[/quote]
yeah now that I've thought it through waiting till the deadline is really important. [quote=BobDBuilder321][quote=MC42]in mafia it's important to share all suspicions, even the faintest ones

because as dust bunny so vehemently asserts, this isn't among us[/quote]Actually, no.

Players tend not to share gut reads - which is essentially reads based of gut, or just what a player [i]feels[/i] like, though you might not have evidence.

And - the reason for this is simple if there's no evidence there's nothing to really discuss and the read has a good chance of being inaccurate.

On other reads that have evidence, however, yeah, it's good to share those. 

Just wanted to correct that you don't have to share your faint gutreads, it's not all that important.[/quote]

post_rendered:
I’ll do a quick POE recap.

The PoE is a group of people who you haven’t found as town yet.

When you find people as town (like, you’re very confident in them being such), you take them out of your POE.

If you take enough townies out of your POE, you can kill everyone in your POE and win, regardless of whether some of them are town or not.

At EoD1 (end of day 1), my POE was {sudz, ct, lulu}. This means I thought everyone else was town. If I was correct in thinking so - my POE was small enough for an auto-win.

Right now, I’ve killed 2 people in my POE. 1 was town, 1 was scum. If my POE is correct, then the last person in it is 100% a wolf. The last person happens to be lulu.

So, first, I want to talk about hanging ct. I hanged ct because they were in my POE. They were the scummiest in my POE, which isn’t saying much because I didn’t have a read on them, but the other person (lulu) was townier. This was sufficient reason to hang ct. (I would like to mention that quickhammering ct the way we did is not “proper” mafia play, you’re ideally supposed to wait for a claim which... in this case would have helped. But, really, no matter how great you guys are, I want this game to end and given ct’s activity levels and also chance of being PR, I thought we would win quicker if we flat out quickhammered ct.)

Now, let’s talk about towncore.

We agree on me, you, dust, joe, and cl being town. This leaves a consensus POE of {bepo, lulu}. This still wins, and so I won’t be too pissed if you end up hanging bepo, but I’m going to try to persuade you otherwise.

I’m also probably going to end up convincing you I’m not guaranteed to be town but :shrug:.

hmmm, this is actually interesting in that both town and scum have valid reasons for doing this for very different reasons
especially early on, town wants broad discussion to encourage analysis and read making, so questioning a lot of people helps achieve that

post_rendered:
Hey, guys.

Some things I wanna talk about.
A. bepo claiming 1sbp and being wolf.

I... was aware of this possibility.

I called bepo a wolf after chris flipped town (in my role PM).

I said bepo should always die if he doesn't get nked (in my role PM).

So, lol to whoever said I was confbiased on bepo and gave him the win.

Now, what did I do wrong on this topic?

Well, I didn't out any of the stuff I said in my role PM about wolves possibly fakeclaiming 1sbp and bepo being a wolf if he didn't die in order to not influence the wolf NK. I was almost 100% sure wolves would shoot bepo if he was 1sbp if I shut up and pretended he was always 1sbp. If I did out that, then frankly, the read wouldn't mean anything because wolves would know they can play around it - this ruins the read.

But, I made the wrong assumption that y'all would realize bepo not dying made him a wolf. You're newbies, it's not expected of you to know this. That's on me.

I do, however, think that mark should have realized this. Not sure why he didn't.
B. calling mc town.

I don't feel bad about this in any regard. I frankly don't think what I did was even close to a throw, nor do I believe my logic was flawed - in the sense that my logic being flawed was because of an oversight, but the logic itself is something I will continue to apply. Essentially, I think my logic was really screwed because of a very small oversight, and I don't feel bad about making said oversight.

On the read. I called MC town because he genuinely felt personally attacked by bunny's attacks on him. I didn't see that coming from a wolf. Like, the dude is wolfreading you for somewhat right-ish (will get to this in a bit) reasons, and you feel attacked. You report him.

That behavior is extremely ??? coming from a wolf, and when I say ???, I mean ???????????????????????????????. It doesn't make sense, and it still doesn't really make sense.

Let me repeat myself. You report someone for attacking you when they're right. You're a wolf. ?????????????????????????????????????????????????

Now, obviously, MC was a wolf. What's wrong with my read?

Well, after talking a bit in specchat, I think the oversight I made was expecting people to make sense. Now, in a normal game, this might be a perfectly reasonable expectation. But, this is a newbie game, and people don't always make sense.

(tl;dr, MC is cw 2.0. I'm sorry for not anticipating another cw could exist, that's on me.) (cw is an absolutely annoying person who's been softbanned by quite a few hosts.)

Also want to talk about some of the wolfreads on MC, and why I don't agree with them.

Ok, bunny's wolfread. I'm sorry for acting like you were just absolutely wrong when you were right, I hope you see why I was so confident. However, I legitimately also didn't see why your reasoning was valid.

It was pretty surface level, and frankly, unconvincing. I still stand by that even though you were right. (tl;dr I think you were right for the wrong reasons.) If you want, I can show you why exactly.

Now, onto one of the particular reads bunny made. This was "MC's treatment of sudz at EoD was extremely partnery". I also saw this "obvious" read repeated multiple times in specchat.

I'm convinced that's specchat bias, they knew MC was wolf from the start and this thus influenced their idea of what's "obvious".

And, I also think it's wrong for one very simple reason. MC's treatment of sudz was very similar to his treatment of ct. Ct was town.

If MC's treatment of sudz was "obviously w/w", I don't see why you can't make the same read for "MC/ct obv w/w".
That's probably all.

Let me repeat myself. You report someone for attacking you when they're right. You're a wolf. ?????????????????????????????????????????????????

Now, obviously, MC was a wolf. What's wrong with my read?

Well, after talking a bit in specchat, I think the oversight I made was expecting people to make sense. Now, in a normal game, this might be a perfectly reasonable expectation. But, this is a newbie game, and people don't always make sense.

(tl;dr, MC is cw 2.0. I'm sorry for not anticipating another cw could exist, that's on me.)

Also want to talk about some of the wolfreads on MC, and why I don't agree with them.

Ok, bunny's wolfread. I'm sorry for acting like you were just absolutely wrong when you were right, I hope you see why I was so confident. However, I legitimately also didn't see why your reasoning was valid.

It was pretty surface level, and frankly, unconvincing. I still stand by that even though you were right. (tl;dr I think you were right for the wrong reasons.) If you want, I can show you why exactly.

Now, onto one of the particular reads bunny made. This was "MC's treatment of sudz at EoD was extremely partnery". I also saw this "obvious" read repeated multiple times in specchat.

I'm convinced that's specchat bias, they knew MC was wolf from the start and this thus influenced their idea of what's "obvious".

And, I also think it's wrong for one very simple reason. MC's treatment of sudz was very similar to his treatment of ct. Ct was town.

If MC's treatment of sudz was "obviously w/w", I don't see why you can't make the same read for "MC/ct obv w/w".
That's probably all.

so your reads were justified
that's nice

post_rendered:
Alright, I should give some feedback while I'm still on break. This is coming from my maybe limited memory of the game, so lmk if anything I say is just wrong.
Chris
I thought your style of asking lots of questions and interacting with others a lot was pretty refreshing to see. I think asking questions is a very valuable tool as town because it can serve two purposes: helping clarify reads for yourself and also helping to determine the alignment of the other person (based on their response). In this game, it seemed like the questions you were asking were mostly to clarify things for yourself. That was fine, but I think you can also try to use your questions to actually "question" other people in future games.
Also, we talked about this in the dead chat a bit, but you should definitely share your thoughts when they come to you! You don't need to always accept what others say as being true, even what they're saying "makes sense" or if they're more experienced. Cuz sometimes the unexpected happens (like the lack of nightkills) and sometimes the experienced are just wrong. And sometimes both happen (like this game).
Also, I would recommend not to vote yourself in the future. I don't think you were completely doomed that day.

flulu
Darn, you were tunneled this game which was unfortunate. I'm sort of confused why that happened, but it may have just been because you got off to a relatively slow start. I think everyone else was just posting a lot and you also entered the game a bit later. When you get tunneled, I recommend not to give up, but just ask people why they're scumreading you and, once you get that reason, do the best you can you can to change your play accordingly.
Another reason why you were scumread was that your posts might have sounded a bit artificial. I think sometimes they came across as matter of fact and not like you were genuinely trying to figure out the game. So I'd recommend taking it slow and interacting with others more.
Also, you had really nice reads, so gj.

joe
Your proactivity to solve the game and interact with others was nice to see. I thought you did a good job getting townread by others because of that proactivity (although not as flashy as having accurate scumreads, getting townread as town is actually a valuable skill to have).
So I think although you did do a good job sharing your thoughts and talking with others, you could have taken more initiative to push your ideas forward. I noticed you didn't vote a lot this game, so I would recommend to vote actively. It's ok to vote wrong; voting is just another form of driving the game forward because it forces people to react (both the person voted and other players as well). Especially since you had bepo as scum later on, it would have been really helpful to the town if you took your stance by voting bepo.

MC
Pretty solid wolf play to be honest. I think you did a good job replicating the genuine sounding posts a townie would make. Next time, people won't give you as much benefit of the doubt, but I think the skill translates.

ct
You didn't have that much time for the game rip. It's surprisingly time consuming.

Hannah
Solid stuff lol.

Bob
You self-diagnosed your issues pretty well. Good job guiding the newbies too, even if you weren't correct.

General Comments
Credit to the SE players for introducing the concept of POE to the newbies. Although it's pretty intuitive once you know what it is, it's a little hard to think of on your own. I think joe and flulu used it really effectively because it basically helped them find the mafia. But perhaps almost too effectively because they didn't seem to have much reasons to actually suspect the scum beyond finding everyone else townie (this might have made it hard for your opinions to have been heard). Ig the point is: you can't rely entirely on POE to win the game because you'll need to find actual reasons in order to successfully hang scum.
Also, I completely agree with what Bob said about this being an incredibly promising batch of newbies. A lot of newbie games have a lot of early inactivity until replacements come in, but this game was really active and enthusiastic early on. I think that's what matters, regardless of whether your reads were good or not (and your reads were pretty good). I see some of you are in other games right now, signed up for upcoming ones, or both. I hope to see every one of you when I return.

Thanks to everyone who played and especially to those who replaced in. For the most part, I thought this was a pretty enjoyable game to spectate, and I hope y'all found it enjoyable too.

ostriches88 wrote:
midwaybear wrote:
Alright, I should give some feedback while I'm still on break. This is coming from my maybe limited memory of the game, so lmk if anything I say is just wrong.
Chris
I thought your style of asking lots of questions and interacting with others a lot was pretty refreshing to see. I think asking questions is a very valuable tool as town because it can serve two purposes: helping clarify reads for yourself and also helping to determine the alignment of the other person (based on their response). In this game, it seemed like the questions you were asking were mostly to clarify things for yourself. That was fine, but I think you can also try to use your questions to actually "question" other people in future games.
Also, we talked about this in the dead chat a bit, but you should definitely share your thoughts when they come to you! You don't need to always accept what others say as being true, even what they're saying "makes sense" or if they're more experienced. Cuz sometimes the unexpected happens (like the lack of nightkills) and sometimes the experienced are just wrong. And sometimes both happen (like this game).
Also, I would recommend not to vote yourself in the future. I don't think you were completely doomed that day.

flulu
Darn, you were tunneled this game which was unfortunate. I'm sort of confused why that happened, but it may have just been because you got off to a relatively slow start. I think everyone else was just posting a lot and you also entered the game a bit later. When you get tunneled, I recommend not to give up, but just ask people why they're scumreading you and, once you get that reason, do the best you can you can to change your play accordingly.
Another reason why you were scumread was that your posts might have sounded a bit artificial. I think sometimes they came across as matter of fact and not like you were genuinely trying to figure out the game. So I'd recommend taking it slow and interacting with others more.
Also, you had really nice reads, so gj.

joe
Your proactivity to solve the game and interact with others was nice to see. I thought you did a good job getting townread by others because of that proactivity (although not as flashy as having accurate scumreads, getting townread as town is actually a valuable skill to have).
So I think although you did do a good job sharing your thoughts and talking with others, you could have taken more initiative to push your ideas forward. I noticed you didn't vote a lot this game, so I would recommend to vote actively. It's ok to vote wrong; voting is just another form of driving the game forward because it forces people to react (both the person voted and other players as well). Especially since you had bepo as scum later on, it would have been really helpful to the town if you took your stance by voting bepo.

MC
Pretty solid wolf play to be honest. I think you did a good job replicating the genuine sounding posts a townie would make. Next time, people won't give you as much benefit of the doubt, but I think the skill translates.

ct
You didn't have that much time for the game rip. It's surprisingly time consuming.

Hannah
Solid stuff lol.

Bob
You self-diagnosed your issues pretty well. Good job guiding the newbies too, even if you weren't correct.

General Comments
Credit to the SE players for introducing the concept of POE to the newbies. Although it's pretty intuitive once you know what it is, it's a little hard to think of on your own. I think joe and flulu used it really effectively because it basically helped them find the mafia. But perhaps almost too effectively because they didn't seem to have much reasons to actually suspect the scum beyond finding everyone else townie (this might have made it hard for your opinions to have been heard). Ig the point is: you can't rely entirely on POE to win the game because you'll need to find actual reasons in order to successfully hang scum.
Also, I completely agree with what Bob said about this being an incredibly promising batch of newbies. A lot of newbie games have a lot of early inactivity until replacements come in, but this game was really active and enthusiastic early on. I think that's what matters, regardless of whether your reads were good or not (and your reads were pretty good). I see some of you are in other games right now, signed up for upcoming ones, or both. I hope to see every one of you when I return.

Thanks to everyone who played and especially to those who replaced in. For the most part, I thought this was a pretty enjoyable game to spectate, and I hope y'all found it enjoyable too.
